verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem cum levare vellet Dominus Eliam per turbinem in cælum, ibant Elias et Eliseus de Galgalis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י בְּהַעֲל֤וֹת יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־אֵ֣לִיָּ֔הוּ בַּֽסְעָרָ֖ה הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם וַיֵּ֧לֶךְ אֵלִיָּ֛הוּ וֶאֱלִישָׁ֖ע מִן־הַגִּלְגָּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass, when the Lord would take up Elias, into heaven, by a whirlwind, that Elias and Eliseus were going from Galgal.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tapahtui, kuin Herra tahtoi Elian tuulispäässä ottaa ylös taivaasen, kävivät Elia ja Elisa Gilgalissa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when the Lord would take up Elijah into heaven by a whirlwind, that Elijah went with Elisha from Gilgal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Elias ad Eliseum : Sede hic, quia Dominus misit me usque in Bethel. Cui ait Eliseus : Vivit Dominus, et vivit anima tua, quia non derelinquam te. Cumque descendissent Bethel,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ אֵלִיָּ֨הוּ אֶל־אֱלִישָׁ֜ע שֵֽׁב־נָ֣א פֹ֗ה כִּ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ שְׁלָחַ֣נִי עַד־בֵּֽית־אֵ֔ל וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלִישָׁ֔ע חַי־יְהֹוָ֥ה וְחֵֽי־נַפְשְׁךָ֖ אִם־אֶעֶזְבֶ֑ךָּ וַיֵּרְד֖וּ בֵּֽית־אֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elias said to Eliseus: Stay thou here, because the Lord hath sent me as far as Bethel. And Eliseus said to him: As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And when they were come down to Bethel,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elia sanoi Elisalle: istus tässä, sillä Herra on minun lähettänyt Beteliin. Mutta Elisa sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää ja sinun sielus elää, en minä luovu sinusta. Ja he tulivat alas Beteliin.
verses.king_james: And Elijah said unto Elisha, Tarry here, I pray thee; for the Lord hath sent me to Beth–el. And Elisha said unto him, As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. So they went down to Beth–el.

verses.latin_vulgate: egressi sunt filii prophetarum qui erant in Bethel, ad Eliseum, et dixerunt ei : Numquid nosti quia hodie Dominus tollet dominum tuum a te ? Qui respondit : Et ego novi : silete.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצְא֨וּ בְנֵי־הַנְּבִיאִ֥ים אֲשֶׁר־בֵּֽית־אֵל֮ אֶל־אֱלִישָׁע֒ וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֔יו הֲיָדַ֕עְתָּ כִּ֣י הַיּ֗וֹם יְהֹוָ֛ה לֹקֵ֥חַ אֶת־אֲדֹנֶ֖יךָ מֵעַ֣ל רֹאשֶׁ֑ךָ וַיֹּ֛אמֶר גַּם־אֲנִ֥י יָדַ֖עְתִּי הֶחֱשֽׁוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The sons of the prophets, that were at Bethel, came forth to Eliseus, and said to him: Dost thou know that, this day, the Lord will take away thy master from thee? And he answered: I also know it: hold your peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin kävivät prophetain pojat, jotka Betelissä olivat, Elisan tykö ja sanoivat hänelle: tiedätkös Herran tänäpänä ottavan sinulta pois isäntäs sinun pääs päältä? Hän sanoi: minä kyllä sen tiedän, olkaat ääneti!
verses.king_james: And the sons of the prophets that were at Beth–el came forth to Elisha, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Lord will take away thy master from thy head to day? And he said, Yea, I know it; hold ye your peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Elias ad Eliseum : Sede hic, quia Dominus misit me in Jericho. Et ille ait : Vivit Dominus, et vivit anima tua, quia non derelinquam te. Cumque venissent Jericho,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ ל֨וֹ אֵלִיָּ֜הוּ אֱלִישָׁ֣ע ׀ שֵֽׁב־נָ֣א פֹ֗ה כִּ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ שְׁלָחַ֣נִי יְרִיח֔וֹ וַיֹּ֕אמֶר חַי־יְהֹוָ֥ה וְחֵֽי־נַפְשְׁךָ֖ אִם־אֶעֶזְבֶ֑ךָּ וַיָּבֹ֖אוּ יְרִיחֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elias said to Eliseus: Stay here, because the Lord hath sent me to Jericho. And he said: As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And when they were come to Jericho,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elia sanoi hänelle: Elisa, viivys tässä, sillä Herra on minun lähettänyt Jerihoon. Hän sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää ja sinun sielus elää, en minä luovu sinusta. Ja niin he tulivat Jerihoon.
verses.king_james: And Elijah said unto him, Elisha, tarry here, I pray thee; for the Lord hath sent me to Jericho. And he said, As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. So they came to Jericho.

verses.latin_vulgate: accesserunt filii prophetarum qui erant in Jericho, ad Eliseum, et dixerunt ei : Numquid nosti quia Dominus hodie tollet dominum tuum a te ? Et ait : Et ego novi : silete.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגְּשׁ֨וּ בְנֵי־הַנְּבִיאִ֥ים אֲשֶׁר־בִּֽירִיחוֹ֮ אֶל־אֱלִישָׁע֒ וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֔יו הֲיָדַ֕עְתָּ כִּ֣י הַיּ֗וֹם יְהֹוָ֛ה לֹקֵ֥חַ אֶת־אֲדֹנֶ֖יךָ מֵעַ֣ל רֹאשֶׁ֑ךָ וַיֹּ֛אמֶר גַּם־אֲנִ֥י יָדַ֖עְתִּי הֶחֱשֽׁוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The sons of the prophets, that were at Jericho, came to Eliseus, and said to him: Dost thou know that, this day, the Lord will take away thy master from thee? And he said: I also know it: hold your peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tulivat prophetain pojat, jotka Jerihossa olivat, Elisan tykö ja sanoivat hänelle: tiedätkös Herran tänäpänä ottavan sinulta pois isäntäs sinun pääs päältä? Hän sanoi: minä kyllä sen tiedän, olkaat ääneti!
verses.king_james: And the sons of the prophets that were at Jericho came to Elisha, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Lord will take away thy master from thy head to day? And he answered, Yea, I know it; hold ye your peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ei Elias : Sede hic, quia Dominus misit me usque ad Jordanem. Qui ait : Vivit Dominus, et vivit anima tua, quia non derelinquam te. Ierunt igitur ambo pariter,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ ל֨וֹ אֵלִיָּ֜הוּ שֵֽׁב־נָ֣א פֹ֗ה כִּ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ שְׁלָחַ֣נִי הַיַּרְדֵּ֔נָה וַיֹּ֕אמֶר חַי־יְהֹוָ֥ה וְחֵֽי־נַפְשְׁךָ֖ אִם־אֶעֶזְבֶ֑ךָּ וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ שְׁנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elias said to him: Stay here, because the Lord hath sent me as far as the Jordan. And he said: as the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And they two went on together.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elia sanoi hänelle: istus tässä, sillä Herra on minun lähettänyt Jordaniin. Hän sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää ja sinun sielus elää, en minä luovu sinusta. Ja he kävivät molemmin.
verses.king_james: And Elijah said unto him, Tarry, I pray thee, here; for the Lord hath sent me to Jordan. And he said, As the Lord liveth, and as thy soul liveth, I will not leave thee. And they two went on.

verses.latin_vulgate: et quinquaginta viri de filiis prophetarum secuti sunt eos, qui et steterunt e contra, longe : illi autem ambo stabant super Jordanem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַחֲמִשִּׁ֨ים אִ֜ישׁ מִבְּנֵ֤י הַנְּבִיאִים֙ הָֽלְכ֔וּ וַיַּעַמְד֥וּ מִנֶּ֖גֶד מֵרָח֑וֹק וּשְׁנֵיהֶ֖ם עָמְד֥וּ עַל־הַיַּרְדֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And fifty men, of the sons of the prophets, followed them, and stood in sight, at a distance: but they two stood by the Jordan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta viisikymmentä miestä prophetain pojista meni pois ja jäivät kauvas seisomaan heidän kohdallansa; vaan he seisoivat ynnä Jordanin tykönä.
verses.king_james: And fifty men of the sons of the prophets went, and stood to view afar off: and they two stood by Jordan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulitque Elias pallium suum, et involvit illud, et percussit aquas : quæ divisæ sunt in utramque partem, et transierunt ambo per siccum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּח֩ אֵלִיָּ֨הוּ אֶת־אַדַּרְתּ֤וֹ וַיִּגְלֹם֙ וַיַּכֶּ֣ה אֶת־הַמַּ֔יִם וַיֵּחָצ֖וּ הֵ֣נָּה וָהֵ֑נָּה וַיַּעַבְר֥וּ שְׁנֵיהֶ֖ם בֶּחָרָבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Elias took his mantle, and folded it together, and struck the waters, and they were divided hither and thither, and they both passed over on dry ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin otti Elia hameensa ja kääri kokoon, ja löi veteen, ja vesi hajosi molemmille puolille, niin että he molemmat kävivät kuivina sen lävitse.
verses.king_james: And Elijah took his mantle, and wrapped it together, and smote the waters, and they were divided hither and thither, so that they two went over on dry ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque transissent, Elias dixit ad Eliseum : Postula quod vis ut faciam tibi, antequam tollar a te. Dixitque Eliseus : Obsecro ut fiat in me duplex spiritus tuus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י כְעׇבְרָ֗ם וְאֵ֨לִיָּ֜הוּ אָמַ֤ר אֶל־אֱלִישָׁע֙ שְׁאַל֙ מָ֣ה אֶעֱשֶׂה־לָּ֔ךְ בְּטֶ֖רֶם אֶלָּקַ֣ח מֵעִמָּ֑ךְ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלִישָׁ֔ע וִ֥יהִי נָ֛א פִּֽי־שְׁנַ֥יִם בְּרוּחֲךָ֖ אֵלָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they were gone over, Elias said to Eliseus: Ask what thou wilt have me to do for thee, before I be taken away from thee. And Eliseus said: I beseech thee, that in me may be thy double spirit.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin he tulivat ylitse, sanoi Elia Elisalle: ano, mitä minun pitää sinulle tekemän, ennenkuin minä otetaan pois sinulta. Elisa sanoi: että kaksi osaa hengestäs olis minun kanssani.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when they were gone over, that Elijah said unto Elisha, Ask what I shall do for thee, before I be taken away from thee. And Elisha said, I pray thee, let a double portion of thy spirit be upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui respondit : Rem difficilem postulasti : attamen si videris me quando tollar a te, erit tibi quod petisti : si autem non videris, non erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר הִקְשִׁ֣יתָ לִשְׁא֑וֹל אִם־תִּרְאֶ֨ה אֹתִ֜י לֻקָּ֤ח מֵֽאִתָּךְ֙ יְהִי־לְךָ֣ כֵ֔ן וְאִם־אַ֖יִן לֹ֥א יִהְיֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he answered: Thou hast asked a hard thing; nevertheless, if thou see me when I am taken from thee, thou shalt have what thou hast asked: but if thou see me not, thou shalt not have it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: sinä olet anonut kovaa asiaa; mutta kuitenkin, jos näet minun kuin minä sinulta otetaan pois, se tapahtuu niin sinulle, mutta jos et näe, niin ei se tapahdu.
verses.king_james: And he said, Thou hast asked a hard thing: nevertheless, if thou see me when I am taken from thee, it shall be so unto thee; but if not, it shall not be so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque pergerent, et incedentes sermocinarentur, ecce currus igneus, et equi ignei diviserunt utrumque : et ascendit Elias per turbinem in cælum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י הֵ֣מָּה הֹלְכִ֤ים הָלוֹךְ֙ וְדַבֵּ֔ר וְהִנֵּ֤ה רֶֽכֶב־אֵשׁ֙ וְס֣וּסֵי אֵ֔שׁ וַיַּפְרִ֖דוּ בֵּ֣ין שְׁנֵיהֶ֑ם וַיַּ֙עַל֙ אֵ֣לִיָּ֔הוּ בַּֽסְעָרָ֖ה הַשָּׁמָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And as they went on, walking and talking together, behold, a fiery chariot and fiery horses parted them both asunder: and Elias went up by a whirlwind into heaven.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui kuin he ynnä kävivät ja puhuivat keskenänsä, katso, niin tulivat tuliset vaunut tulisten hevosten kanssa ja eroittivat heidät toinen toisestansa. Ja Elia meni ylös tuulispäässä taivaasen.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as they still went on, and talked, that, behold, there appeared a chariot of fire, and horses of fire, and parted them both asunder; and Elijah went up by a whirlwind into heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eliseus autem videbat, et clamabat : Pater mi, pater mi, currus Israël, et auriga ejus. Et non vidit eum amplius : apprehenditque vestimenta sua, et scidit illa in duas partes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וֶאֱלִישָׁ֣ע רֹאֶ֗ה וְה֤וּא מְצַעֵק֙ אָבִ֣י ׀ אָבִ֗י רֶ֤כֶב יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וּפָ֣רָשָׁ֔יו וְלֹ֥א רָאָ֖הוּ ע֑וֹד וַֽיַּחֲזֵק֙ בִּבְגָדָ֔יו וַיִּקְרָעֵ֖ם לִשְׁנַ֥יִם קְרָעִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliseus saw him, and cried: My father, my father, the chariot of Israel, and the driver thereof. And he saw him no more: and he took hold of his own garments, and rent them in two pieces.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Elisa näki sen ja huusi: minun isäni, minun isäni, Israelin vaunu ja hänen ratsasmiehensä! ja ei häntä enää nähnyt. Ja hän tarttui vaatteisiinsa ja repäisi ne kahtia,
verses.king_james: And Elisha saw it, and he cried, My father, my father, the chariot of Israel, and the horsemen thereof. And he saw him no more: and he took hold of his own clothes, and rent them in two pieces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et levavit pallium Eliæ, quod ceciderat ei : reversusque stetit super ripam Jordanis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֙רֶם֙ אֶת־אַדֶּ֣רֶת אֵלִיָּ֔הוּ אֲשֶׁ֥ר נָפְלָ֖ה מֵעָלָ֑יו וַיָּ֥שׇׁב וַֽיַּעֲמֹ֖ד עַל־שְׂפַ֥ת הַיַּרְדֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took up the mantle of Elias, that fell from him: and going back, he stood on the bank of the Jordan;
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja otti Elian hameen ylös, joka häneltä pudonnut oli, palasi ja seisoi Jordanin rannalla;
verses.king_james: He took up also the mantle of Elijah that fell from him, and went back, and stood by the bank of Jordan;

verses.latin_vulgate: et pallio Eliæ, quod ceciderat ei, percussit aquas, et non sunt divisæ : et dixit : Ubi est Deus Eliæ etiam nunc ? Percussitque aquas, et divisæ sunt huc atque illuc, et transiit Eliseus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּח֩ אֶת־אַדֶּ֨רֶת אֵלִיָּ֜הוּ אֲשֶׁר־נָפְלָ֤ה מֵֽעָלָיו֙ וַיַּכֶּ֣ה אֶת־הַמַּ֔יִם וַיֹּאמַ֕ר אַיֵּ֕ה יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵ֣י אֵלִיָּ֑הוּ אַף־ה֣וּא ׀ וַיַּכֶּ֣ה אֶת־הַמַּ֗יִם וַיֵּֽחָצוּ֙ הֵ֣נָּה וָהֵ֔נָּה וַֽיַּעֲבֹ֖ר אֱלִישָֽׁע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he struck the waters with the mantle of Elias, that had fallen from him, and they were not divided. And he said: Where is now the God of Elias? And he struck the waters, and they were divided hither and thither, and Eliseus passed over.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja otti Elian hameen, joka häneltä pudonnut oli, löi veteen ja sanoi: kussa on nyt Herra Elian Jumala? ja löi veteen, niin vesi hajosi molemmille puolille, ja Elisa kävi ylitse.
verses.king_james: And he took the mantle of Elijah that fell from him, and smote the waters, and said, Where is the Lord God of Elijah? and when he also had smitten the waters, they parted hither and thither: and Elisha went over.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem filii prophetarum qui erant in Jericho e contra, dixerunt : Requievit spiritus Eliæ super Eliseum. Et venientes in occursum ejus, adoraverunt eum proni in terram,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּרְאֻ֨הוּ בְנֵי־הַנְּבִיאִ֤ים אֲשֶׁר־בִּֽירִיחוֹ֙ מִנֶּ֔גֶד וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ נָ֛חָה ר֥וּחַ אֵלִיָּ֖הוּ עַל־אֱלִישָׁ֑ע וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ לִקְרָאת֔וֹ וַיִּשְׁתַּחֲווּ־ל֖וֹ אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the sons of the prophets, at Jericho, who were over against him, seeing it, said: The spirit of Elias hath rested upon Eliseus. And coming to meet him, they worshipped him, falling to the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin prophetain pojat, jotka toisella puolella Jerihossa olivat, näkivät hänen, sanoivat he: Elian henki lepää Elisan päällä, ja kävivät häntä vastaan ja kumarsivat maahan asti,
verses.king_james: And when the sons of the prophets which were to view at Jericho saw him, they said, The spirit of Elijah doth rest on Elisha. And they came to meet him, and bowed themselves to the ground before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixeruntque illi : Ecce cum servis tuis sunt quinquaginta viri fortes qui possunt ire, et quærere dominum tuum, ne forte tulerit eum spiritus Domini, et projecerit eum in unum montium, aut in unam vallium. Qui ait : Nolite mittere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֡יו הִנֵּה־נָ֣א יֵֽשׁ־אֶת־עֲבָדֶ֩יךָ֩ חֲמִשִּׁ֨ים אֲנָשִׁ֜ים בְּנֵי־חַ֗יִל יֵ֣לְכוּ נָא֮ וִיבַקְשׁ֣וּ אֶת־אֲדֹנֶ֒יךָ֒ פֶּן־נְשָׂאוֹ֙ ר֣וּחַ יְהֹוָ֔ה וַיַּשְׁלִכֵ֙הוּ֙ בְּאַחַ֣ד הֶהָרִ֔ים א֖וֹ בְּאַחַ֣ת (הגיאות) [הַגֵּיאָי֑וֹת] וַיֹּ֖אמֶר לֹ֥א תִשְׁלָֽחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they said to him: Behold, there are with thy servants, fifty strong men, that can go, and seek thy master, lest, perhaps, the spirit of the Lord, hath taken him up and cast him upon some mountain, or into some valley. And he said: Do not send.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoivat hänelle: katso, sinun palvelioissas on viisikymmentä vahvaa miestä, anna heidän mennä etsimään isäntääsi, jos Herran henki on ottanut hänen ja heittänyt jollekulle vuorelle, taikka johonkuhun laaksoon. Hän sanoi: älkäät antako mennä!
verses.king_james: And they said unto him, Behold now, there be with thy servants fifty strong men; let them go, we pray thee, and seek thy master: lest peradventure the Spirit of the Lord hath taken him up, and cast him upon some mountain, or into some valley. And he said, Ye shall not send.

verses.latin_vulgate: Coëgeruntque eum donec acquiesceret, et diceret : Mittite. Et miserunt quinquaginta viros : qui cum quæsissent tribus diebus, non invenerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְצְרוּ־ב֥וֹ עַד־בֹּ֖שׁ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר שְׁלָ֑חוּ וַֽיִּשְׁלְחוּ֙ חֲמִשִּׁ֣ים אִ֔ישׁ וַיְבַקְשׁ֥וּ שְׁלֹשָֽׁה־יָמִ֖ים וְלֹ֥א מְצָאֻֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But they pressed him, till he consented, and said: Send. And they sent fifty men: and they sought three days, but found him not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta he vaativat häntä siihenasti, että hän häpesi, ja sanoi: antakaat mennä! Ja he lähettivät matkaan viisikymmentä miestä, ja he etsivät kolme päivää ja ei löytäneet häntä,
verses.king_james: And when they urged him till he was ashamed, he said, Send. They sent therefore fifty men; and they sought three days, but found him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversi sunt ad eum : at ille habitabat in Jericho, et dixit eis : Numquid non dixi vobis : Nolite mittere ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּשֻׁ֣בוּ אֵלָ֔יו וְה֖וּא יֹשֵׁ֣ב בִּירִיח֑וֹ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֲלֵהֶ֔ם הֲלֹֽא־אָמַ֥רְתִּי אֲלֵיכֶ֖ם אַל־תֵּלֵֽכוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And they came back to him: for he abode at Jericho, and he said to them: Did I not say to you? Do not send.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tulivat jälleen hänen tykönsä, ja hän asui Jerihossa; ja hän sanoi heille: enkö minä sanonut teille, ettei teidän pitänyt menemän?
verses.king_james: And when they came again to him, (for he tarried at Jericho,) he said unto them, Did I not say unto you, Go not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt quoque viri civitatis ad Eliseum : Ecce habitatio civitatis hujus optima est, sicut tu ipse, domine, perspicis : sed aquæ pessimæ sunt, et terra sterilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמְר֜וּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י הָעִיר֙ אֶל־אֱלִישָׁ֔ע הִנֵּה־נָ֞א מוֹשַׁ֤ב הָעִיר֙ ט֔וֹב כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר אֲדֹנִ֖י רֹאֶ֑ה וְהַמַּ֥יִם רָעִ֖ים וְהָאָ֥רֶץ מְשַׁכָּֽלֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the men of the city, said to Eliseus . Behold the situation of this city is very good, as thou, my lord, seest: but the waters are very bad, and the ground barren.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaupungin miehet sanoivat Elisalle: katso, tässä kaupungissa on hyvä asua, niinkuin meidän herramme näkee; mutta vesi on paha ja maa hedelmätöin.
verses.king_james: And the men of the city said unto Elisha, Behold, I pray thee, the situation of this city is pleasant, as my lord seeth: but the water is naught, and the ground barren.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille ait : Afferte mihi vas novum, et mittite in illud sal. Quod cum attulissent,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר קְחוּ־לִי֙ צְלֹחִ֣ית חֲדָשָׁ֔ה וְשִׂ֥ימוּ שָׁ֖ם מֶ֑לַח וַיִּקְח֖וּ אֵלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Bring me a new vessel, and put salt into it. And when they had brought it,
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: tuokaat minulle uusi astia ja pankaat suolaa siihen! Ja he toivat sen hänelle.
verses.king_james: And he said, Bring me a new cruse, and put salt therein. And they brought it to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: egressus ad fontem aquarum misit in illum sal, et ait : Hæc dicit Dominus : Sanavi aquas has, et non erit ultra in eis mors, neque sterilitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵא֙ אֶל־מוֹצָ֣א הַמַּ֔יִם וַיַּשְׁלֶךְ־שָׁ֖ם מֶ֑לַח וַיֹּ֜אמֶר כֹּה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה רִפִּ֙אתִי֙ לַמַּ֣יִם הָאֵ֔לֶּה לֹא־יִהְיֶ֥ה מִשָּׁ֛ם ע֖וֹד מָ֥וֶת וּמְשַׁכָּֽלֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: He went out to the spring of the waters, and cast the salt into it, and said: Thus saith the Lord: I have healed these waters, and there shall be no more in them death or barrenness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän meni ulos vesihuokoin tykö ja heitti suolaa niihin, sanoen: näin sanoo Herra: minä olen tämän veden parantanut, ei kuolema, taikka hedelmättömyys pidä heistä tästedes tuleman.
verses.king_james: And he went forth unto the spring of the waters, and cast the salt in there, and said, Thus saith the Lord, I have healed these waters; there shall not be from thence any more death or barren land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanatæ sunt ergo aquæ usque in diem hanc, juxta verbum Elisei quod locutus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּרָפ֣וּ הַמַּ֔יִם עַ֖ד הַיּ֣וֹם הַזֶּ֑ה כִּדְבַ֥ר אֱלִישָׁ֖ע אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֵּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the waters were healed unto this day, according to the word of Eliseus, which he spoke.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin parani vesi tähän päivään asti Elisan sanan jälkeen, jonka hän puhui.
verses.king_james: So the waters were healed unto this day, according to the saying of Elisha which he spake.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit autem inde in Bethel : cumque ascenderet per viam, pueri parvi egressi sunt de civitate, et illudebant ei, dicentes : Ascende calve, ascende calve.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַל מִשָּׁ֖ם בֵּֽית־אֵ֑ל וְה֣וּא ׀ עֹלֶ֣ה בַדֶּ֗רֶךְ וּנְעָרִ֤ים קְטַנִּים֙ יָצְא֣וּ מִן־הָעִ֔יר וַיִּתְקַלְּסוּ־בוֹ֙ וַיֹּ֣אמְרוּ ל֔וֹ עֲלֵ֥ה קֵרֵ֖חַ עֲלֵ֥ה קֵרֵֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he went up from thence to Bethel: and as he was going up by the way, little boys came out of the city and mocked him, saying: Go up, thou bald head, go up, thou bald head.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän meni sieltä ylös Beteliin, ja hänen mennessänsä tiellä, tulivat pienet pojat kaupungista ja pilkkasivat häntä, sanoen hänelle: tule ylös paljaspää, tule ylös paljaspää!
verses.king_james: And he went up from thence unto Beth–el: and as he was going up by the way, there came forth little children out of the city, and mocked him, and said unto him, Go up, thou bald head; go up, thou bald head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum respexisset, vidit eos, et maledixit eis in nomine Domini : egressique sunt duo ursi de saltu, et laceraverunt ex eis quadraginta duos pueros.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֤פֶן אַֽחֲרָיו֙ וַיִּרְאֵ֔ם וַֽיְקַלְלֵ֖ם בְּשֵׁ֣ם יְהֹוָ֑ה וַתֵּצֶ֜אנָה שְׁתַּ֤יִם דֻּבִּים֙ מִן־הַיַּ֔עַר וַתְּבַקַּ֣עְנָה מֵהֶ֔ם אַרְבָּעִ֥ים וּשְׁנֵ֖י יְלָדִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And looking back, he saw them, and cursed them in the name of the Lord: and there came forth two bears out of the forest, and tore of them, two and forty boys.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän käänsi itsensä, ja kuin hän heidät näki, kirosi hän heitä Herran nimeen. Niin tuli kaksi karhua metsästä ja repeli heistä kaksi poikaa viidettäkymmentä kuoliaaksi.
verses.king_james: And he turned back, and looked on them, and cursed them in the name of the Lord. And there came forth two she bears out of the wood, and tare forty and two children of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit autem inde in montem Carmeli, et inde reversus est in Samariam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ מִשָּׁ֖ם אֶל־הַ֣ר הַכַּרְמֶ֑ל וּמִשָּׁ֖ם שָׁ֥ב שֹׁמְרֽוֹן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And from thence he went to mount Carmel, and from thence he returned to Samaria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sieltä hän meni Karmelin vuorelle, ja palasi sieltä sitten Samariaan.
verses.king_james: And he went from thence to mount Carmel, and from thence he returned to Samaria.
